genproc 0.0.0.9000

genproc: robust, logged and reproducible iteration at organizational scale

Source: R/genproc-package.R
genproc-package.Rd

The genproc package wraps classical iteration constructs (for, base::lapply(), purrr::pmap(), ...) with orthogonal execution layers so that one-off scripts can be turned into production-grade procedures without restructuring the user's code.

Details

Two layers are always active (they cannot be disabled):

  • Logged: each case produces a structured log row with the real traceback (captured through withCallingHandlers()) and timing.

  • Reproducibility: each run records the R version, loaded package versions, execution environment, the exact mask used, and hashes of the file inputs referenced by the mask, so that silent non-reproducibility between runs can be flagged to the user.

Further layers are planned (parallel, non-blocking, monitored, error replay). They will be composable with the default layers and opt-in.

This package has no Shiny dependency and is intended to be reusable from a future companion Shiny package.

See also

Useful links:

  • https://github.com/danielrak/genproc

  • https://danielrak.github.io/genproc/

  • Report bugs at https://github.com/danielrak/genproc/issues
